Six years ago, Ifeanyi Okereke, a newspaper vendor, was shot dead in Abuja by a security aide attached to Femi Gbajabiamila in Central Business District Abuja. At the time of his death, his wife had just given birth to their child barely two weeks earlier.   A young family was shattered, a father was taken away, and a future was stolen. Years later, many Nigerians still ask whether justice was ever truly served.   Like countless other cases, this tragedy appears to have been pushed aside, leaving the victim’s family to bear the pain alone while those connected to power continued their careers unhindered.  For many, Femi’s name has been associated with controversy for far too long. The memory of Ifeanyi Okereke should not be forgotten, nor should the cries of a widow and a child who lost a husband and father.  A nation cannot build a just future by burying painful truths and abandoning victims. The culture of impunity, where the powerful move on while ordinary citizens suffer in silence, is the very structure that must be dismantled if Nigeria is to become a country where justice truly matters.

Last Monday afternoon, something happened—captured by security cameras at the Pine Hollow animal shelter in Bowling Green, Kentucky—that employees say they still cannot watch without crying. The man in the video is Walter. He is fifty-eight years old. Three months ago, he retired after working as a mail carrier for thirty-two years. For decades, his life had been defined by a precise routine: early mornings, delivery routes, familiar streets, familiar faces. Every day had a shape and a purpose. Then, suddenly, retirement arrived. His wife, Denise, later said that the silence in the house began to affect him almost immediately. Walter wasn't unhappy; he was just... adrift, without his usual points of reference. For weeks, he had been visiting a local dog shelter; one day, on his own, he found a dog that was even lonelier than he was, and they formed a bond the likes of which is rarely seen. So, we find ourselves asking: who saved whom?

AI reality: Bring the AI capability to your data rather than your data to the AI.  AI-ready data has to meet four requirements: It must be: ▫️ Discoverable (Having a single view of what exists across the whole estate.) ▫️ Governed (Well-enforced rules about who can see what, and these rules must travel with the data wherever it goes. ) ▫️ Current (A change at the source shows up downstream without reprocessing everything from scratch.) ▫️ Safe to use (A model gets all of the relevant data and none of the material it should never see.)  Every AI project is a data decision. While compute gets the headlines, the reality is that every AI initiative is fundamentally a data project. It is a decision about future pricing, data governance, and data control. ▫️ Snapshots ▫️ Replication ▫️ Backup patterns ▫️ Data services ▫️ Recovery models ▫️ Operational runbooks  If data is your most valuable asset, then storage strategy cannot be treated like a footnote.  For decades, data storage was judged on capacity and performance (how much it could hold, the cost per terabyte, how fast you could reach it).  Agentic AI changes the question being asked of storage.  Storage is:  ▫️ Resilience ▫️ Recoverability ▫️ Compliance ▫️ Ransomware blast-radius control  Security and recovery cannot be bolted on after the data is already feeding models, so they have to belong to the layer the data lives in.  The instinct is to copy data to wherever the AI runs, and copying carries real costs. Lineage breaks, permissions drift, spend multiplies, and every copy becomes one more thing to secure. It is not unusual to end up with several copies of the same file scattered around, with the same name, same date, and no clear record of which one to trust.  By utilizing a consistent data platform across on-premises and cloud environments, enterprises can maintain data control, reduce replication costs, and ensure security without creating risky, fragmented data copies.  The #NetApp #ONTAP data fabric enables this, providing essential data services and ransomware protection.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Breach of Brazilian FX Broker VIPS  A threat actor operating under the alias 'Kaido' claims to have breached VIPS Corretora de Cambio SA, a Brazilian foreign exchange brokerage regulated by the Central Bank of Brazil (BACEN). According to a post on an underground forum, the actor alleges they possess complete backend access and sensitive records from the company, which operates under the domains vipscambio.com.br and vipscc.com.br.  The post enumerates a significant volume of compromised data and assets, allegedly including:  *   115,109 unique CPFs (Brazilian taxpayer IDs) *   2,414 client bank accounts with full details *   72 internal system credentials *   3,595 signed contracts (DocuSign) *   18 corporate bank accounts *   Digital certificates with passwords *   Remote access to production servers *   Government reporting credentials  Beyond personal data, the actor alleges exposure of critical financial intelligence, claiming evidence of R$ 330 million in unreported Anti-Money Laundering (AML) operations. The post also cites financial movement logs showing R$ 262 million in transactions over a 60-day period, alongside monthly FX volumes of R$ 130 million. Additionally, the actor claims to have exposed full PII for five directors and system access details for 72 employees.  VIPS Corretora de Cambio SA is a licensed financial institution in Brazil, making it subject to strict regulatory oversight by BACEN. If authentic, the alleged exposure of unreported AML operations and client financial data could result in severe regulatory penalties and reputational damage for the firm. The actor provided sample files via privatebin.net links to substantiate the claims.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by VIPS or regulatory authorities.

⚠️ Threat Actor Claims Massive Data Leak of Grupo ATC and Major Auto Partners  A threat actor has allegedly leaked over 340 GB of data from Grupo ATC, a Mexican logistics and transportation conglomerate, along with sensitive information pertaining to dozens of major global corporations. According to a post on an underground forum, the actor claims to have obtained 23 databases containing more than 2 billion unique rows after negotiations for a ransom failed. The actor alleges that because the company did not pay, they are now releasing the data to the public.  The leak reportedly includes highly sensitive credentials and personally identifiable information (PII) related to Grupo ATC’s internal operations and its extensive network of partners. The actor claims the following categories of data are exposed:  *   OAuth2 bearer/refresh tokens and JWT tokens *   Cleartext application passwords and SFTP credentials *   API credentials and third-party API keys *   Password hashes (bcrypt $2a$11$, PBKDF2, custom salted) *   Email reset tokens and intercepted business emails *   Employee PII including names, emails, phone numbers, RFCs, and bank account numbers (CLABES) *   GPS position data and operational freight routes *   Vehicle identification numbers with routing information *   Internal source code paths and cloud storage account names  The actor asserts that the breach extends beyond Grupo ATC, exposing data linked to major automotive and manufacturing giants such as Ford, Toyota, Tesla, Honda, Nissan, Mazda, Hyundai, BMW, General Motors, Stellantis, Penske, John Deere, Nestle, Mabe, and L'Oréal. The post suggests this data could be used for spear-phishing, fraud, or gaining initial access for further ransomware campaigns against these high-profile entities.  Grupo ATC is a significant player in the North American logistics sector, specializing in automotive logistics and supply chain solutions. If authentic, the exposure of internal API endpoints, freight billing amounts, and employee financial details could facilitate targeted attacks on both the logistics provider and its corporate clients. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Sale of 4M ISSSTE Records on Darkweb  A listing on an underground forum alleges the sale of a historical database containing over 4 million records from Mexico’s Institute of Social Security and Social Services for State Workers (ISSSTE). The post, dated July 1, 2026, claims to offer access to sensitive personal and employment data belonging to state employees.  According to the thread, the dataset includes a wide range of personally identifiable information (PII) and financial details. The actor lists the following data fields as being present in the dump:  *   CURP (Unique Population Registry Code) *   RFC (Federal Taxpayer Identification Code) *   NSS (Social Security Number) *   ISSSTE Number *   Full names (first name, father's last name, mother's last name) *   Salary details (ISSSTE salary, SAR salary, total compensation) *   Employment history (start date, appointment type, enrollment date) *   Administrative identifiers (branch number, payroll office number, payment code)  ISSSTE is a critical government entity in Mexico, providing social security benefits to millions of federal public servants. The exposure of such comprehensive employment and tax data could facilitate identity theft, social engineering attacks, and targeted fraud against state workers. The post indicates that the price for the database is negotiable and directs interested buyers to contact the seller via Signal.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or official statements from ISSSTE.

🚨 Alleged DETRAN SP Database Leak Exposes 13M Brazilian Records  A darkweb forum post claims the unauthorized release of a database containing personal information for 13 million individuals from DETRAN-SP, the Department of Motor Vehicles of São Paulo, Brazil. The listing, dated June 29, 2026, offers the data as a "FREE DB" in .db format, with a file size of 1.59 GB. The actor provides a download link via gofile.io and includes sample records to demonstrate the content.  The exposed dataset allegedly contains sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) for residents of São Paulo. According to the SQL schema and sample rows provided in the post, the database includes:  *   Full names and social names *   CPF numbers (Brazilian individual taxpayer registry) *   RG numbers (national identity documents) and issuing authorities *   Dates of birth and gender *   Full residential addresses, including street, number, neighborhood, city, and ZIP codes *   Telephone numbers (landline and mobile) *   Email addresses *   Driver’s license status and registration dates *   Photographs of license holders (referenced by filename) *   Marital status and father's name  DETRAN-SP is one of the largest vehicle registration agencies in Latin America, serving the state of São Paulo, which has a population exceeding 46 million people. A breach of this magnitude would represent a significant exposure of citizen data, potentially facilitating identity theft, fraud, and targeted phishing campaigns against millions of Brazilians.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and there is no official confirmation from DETRAN-SP or Brazilian law enforcement regarding the incident.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Full Takeover of AWS Wickr GovCloud Infrastructure  A threat actor operating under the alias 'Orcinus orca' has claimed to have taken over the production infrastructure of Wickr's military GovCloud environment hosted on Amazon Web Services (AWS). According to a post on an underground forum, the group alleges they have extracted and are leaking the full production container, including unstripped binaries, cryptographic SDKs, and internal configuration files.  The actor claims the leaked repository contains sensitive technical artifacts, including: *   The main server executable (wickrio_compliance) and a 161MB MLS Cryptographic SDK (libWickrMlsSdkCpp.so), allegedly left unstripped with full symbol tables and cryptographic routing flows exposed. *   74 production runtime libraries, purportedly exposing internal AWS C++ SDK extensions for S3, DynamoDB, and KMS orchestration. *   Raw environment JSON maps and cryptographic configurations (fips.so, openssl.cnf), which the actor claims verify hardcoded local service flags.  The post further alleges that the actor audited the binaries and found up to 22,244 writable GOT.PLT entries exposed due to a "flawed partial RELRO configuration." The actor invites security researchers to analyze the dump using tools like Ghidra or IDA Pro to verify the claims. Additionally, Orcinus orca announced plans to sell 14 zero-day vulnerabilities targeting the ksmbd module, one zero-day for libjs, and one QEMU guest-to-host escape zero-day.  Wickr is a secure messaging platform acquired by Amazon in 2019, widely used by government agencies and military organizations for encrypted communications. A breach of its GovCloud infrastructure could have significant implications for national security and defense communications if the alleged access to production environments is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Amazon or Wickr.  #DataBreach #Wickr #AWS #GovCloud #OrcinusOrca #ZeroDay #CyberSecurity

🇬🇧 A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a full database belonging to UK-based engineering firm AMS Group (ams-group.co.uk).  According to the post, the archive is approximately 17.14 GB and allegedly contains: * Administrative and financial records * Payroll documents * Client and partner directories * Employee records * Engineering specifications and reports * Architectural designs and construction site maps * Contracts, legal and tax documents * Internal correspondence and business plans  At the time of writing, the authenticity of the dataset and the claimed source have not been independently verified.  Analyst Note: If genuine, this type of exposure extends far beyond personal data. Engineering documents, contracts, internal communications, and project information can be leveraged for corporate espionage, supply chain attacks, targeted phishing, and business email compromise (BEC).  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #UK

🇫🇷 The Sport 2000 customer database has reportedly resurfaced on a cybercrime forum as a free download.  According to the forum post: * ~150,000 customer records * Full names * Email addresses * Mobile phone numbers * Postal addresses * Dates of birth * Loyalty card and membership details * Purchase history, reward points, and marketing preferences  The dataset is claimed to originate from the April 2024 data breach affecting French sporting goods retailer Sport 2000.  Analyst Note: Previously sold datasets frequently reappear as free downloads, greatly expanding access for cybercriminals. Even older customer records remain valuable for phishing campaigns, identity fraud, account takeover attempts, and social engineering.  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #France

🇱🇰 A threat actor is advertising what they claim is a database containing 100,000 records allegedly sourced from Sri Lanka's Department of Agriculture.  According to the post, the dataset includes: * Full names * Telephone numbers * Addresses * District information * Language preferences * Additional registration-related fields  At the time of writing, the authenticity of the dataset and the claimed source have not been independently verified.  Analyst Note: Government databases are frequently abused for phishing, social engineering, and identity fraud. If confirmed, affected organizations should investigate the claim, determine the source of the exposure, and notify impacted individuals where appropriate.  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #SriLanka

🇫🇷 Coriolis Telecom customer database has reportedly resurfaced on a cybercrime forum as a free download.  According to the forum post: * ~508,276 customer records * Names, email addresses, phone numbers * Physical addresses * Dates of birth * Gender * IBAN/bank account numbers (for many records)  The dataset appears to originate from the August 2025 breach of the French ISP, where the data was initially offered for sale before allegedly being leaked publicly.  Analyst Note: Data that was once sold on underground forums often becomes freely available months later, significantly increasing the risk of identity theft, phishing, financial fraud, and credential enrichment attacks. Organizations should assume threat actors now have broad access to the dataset.  #DDW #DarkWeb #Intelligence #France

☎️ Alleged Exposure: Vodafone Consumer Telesales Credentials  A threat actor claims to have obtained internal connection information related to Vodafone's Consumer Telesales operations, allegedly involving EMERGIACC España, which is described as a strategic partner.  According to the post, the exposed information reportedly includes:  * Consumer telesales account details * Internal usernames * Agency and operational profile information * Backend access references * Internal connection details  The threat actor also shared what appears to be sample employee account information to support the claim.  At the time of writing, these allegations remain unverified. The authenticity of the data and whether any unauthorized access occurred have not been independently confirmed.  Analyst Note: Third-party vendors and outsourcing partners continue to represent a significant attack surface. Even when a primary organization is not directly compromised, exposure of partner credentials can create opportunities for unauthorized access, phishing, or lateral movement across connected environments.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Vodafone

🇵🇦 Alleged Data Leak: Panama's Social Security Fund (CSS)  A threat actor is claiming to have obtained and listed internal documents allegedly belonging to Panama's Caja de Seguro Social (CSS).  According to the listing, the exposed data reportedly includes:  * Call center statistics (2019–2020) * Board agreements and internal documents * Medication and drug inventory records * Approved pharmaceutical supplier lists * Pension and payroll statistics * Human resources training and licensing data * Medical supply inventories * Identification-related records  At the time of writing, these claims remain unverified. The authenticity, scope, and impact of the alleged dataset have not been independently confirmed.  Analyst Note: Government and healthcare organizations continue to be attractive targets due to the large volumes of sensitive operational and citizen data they maintain. Organizations should monitor for official statements, validate any exposed records, and assess potential downstream risks if the claims are confirmed.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Panama

Victims are tricked into opening a weaponized ZIP archive that deploys a Go-based Windows backdoor. llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-03 08:06 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI 기술을 활용하여 공격 효율을 극대화하고 있으며, 특히 LLM의 환각(Hallucination) 현상을 악용한 'Phantom Squatting' 및 AI Compute Hijacking이 새로운 위협으로 부상하고 있습니다. 또한, Citrix Bleed 2(CVE-2025-5777)와 같은 고위험 취약점이 공개 직후 즉각적으로 악용되는 'Zero-day' 공격 주기가 매우 짧아졌습니다. 공급망 공격과 더불어 MFA 우회를 노리는 ConsentFix, ClickFix와 같은 정교한 사회공학적 기법이 기업의 클라우드 환경을 지속적으로 위협하고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Anubis Ransomware**: Citrix Bleed 2(CVE-2025-5777)를 악용하여 초기 침투 수행 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/ransomware-groups-turn-to-citrix-bleed.html))
*   **INC & Lynx Ransomware**: FortiBleed 캠페인을 통해 FortiGate 방화벽 자격 증명을 탈취하고 랜섬웨어 공격 수행 ([SecurityWeek](https://www.securityweek.com/fortibleed-campaign-linked-to-inc-lynx-ransomware-attacks/))
*   **TAG-182 (Iran-nexus)**: MarkiRAT 악성코드를 사용하여 VPN 및 미디어 앱으로 위장, 국내 타겟 감시 수행 ([RecordedFuture](https://www.recordedfuture.com/research/nexus-tag182-disseminates-markirat))
*   **Scattered Spider**: Transport for London 공격 관련 주요 인물 체포 및 유죄 판결 ([Krebs on Security](https://krebsonsecurity.com/2026/06/scattered-spider-hackers-plead-guilty-on-day-1-of-trial/))

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **Settra**: 한국 산업장비 제조 대기업의 해외 법인 데이터 유출 주장 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94314/))
*   **Kimsuky**: 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 지속 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5765))
*   **기타**: 자위대 USB 악성코드 혼입 문제와 관련하여 일본 내 보안 조사 강화 언급 ([Twitter](https://twitter.com/securityLab_jp/status/2072802998472851504#m))

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2025-5777**: Citrix Bleed 2, 고위험, 현재 랜섬웨어 그룹에 의해 활발히 악용 중.
*   **CVE-2026-8451**: Citrix, 공개 24시간 이내 악용 사례 보고됨.
*   **CVE-2026-59097**: Taiga, 인증 우회 취약점, 원격 공격 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-59101**: AutoBangumi, SSRF 취약점, 내부망 탐색 가능.
*   **CVE-2026-58467**: Cockpit CMS, 경로 탐색 및 로컬 파일 포함(LFI) 취약점.

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE**:
    *   CVE-2025-5777 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/ransomware-groups-turn-to-citrix-bleed.html))
    *   CVE-2026-8451 ([TheHackersNews](https://twitter.com/TheHackersNews/status/2072712049768190112#m))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **Citrix/Fortinet 패치**: Citrix Bleed 2(CVE-2025-5777) 및 FortiGate 관련 취약점에 대한 최신 보안 패치 즉시 적용.
*   **MFA 강화**: Microsoft 365 환경에서 ClickFix/ConsentFix 공격 방지를 위해 OAuth 앱 권한 승인 프로세스 재검토 및 조건부 액세스 정책 강화.
*   **공급망 보안**: npm 패키지 등 오픈소스 라이브러리 사용 시 악성 패키지(예: chai-as-init) 여부 검증 및 의존성 관리 강화.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 8.5/10
*   **근거**: 공개된 취약점(Citrix, Fortinet)이 랜섬웨어 그룹에 의해 즉각적으로 악용되고 있으며, 공급망 및 AI 기반 공격이 고도화되어 기업 인프라 전반에 걸친 즉각적인 대응이 요구됨.
